ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Gene sets are fundamental for gene enrichment analysis. The package 'geneset' enables querying gene sets from public databases including 'GO' (Gene Ontology Consortium. (2004) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkh036>), 'KEGG' (Minoru et al. (2000) <doi:10.1093/nar/28.1.27>), 'WikiPathway' (Marvin et al. (2020) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkaa1024>), 'MsigDb' (Arthur et al. (2015) <doi:10.1016/j.cels.2015.12.004>), 'Reactome' (David et al. (2011) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkq1018>), 'MeSH' (Ish et al. (2014) <doi:10.4103/0019-5413.139827>), 'DisGeNET' (Janet et al. (2017) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkw943>), 'Disease Ontology' (Lynn et al. (2011) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkr972>), 'Network of Cancer Genes' (Dimitra et al. (2019) <doi:10.1186/s13059-018-1612-0>) and 'COVID-19' (Maxim et al. (2020) <doi:10.21203/rs.3.rs-28582/v1>). Gene sets are stored in the list object which provides data frame of 'geneset' and 'geneset_name'. The 'geneset' has two columns of term ID and gene ID. The 'geneset_name' has two columns of terms ID and term description.

Gene sets are fundamental for gene enrichment analysis. The package 'geneset' enables querying gene sets from public databases including 'GO' (Gene Ontology Consortium. (2004) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkh036>), 'KEGG' (Minoru et al. (2000) <doi:10.1093/nar/28.1.27>), 'WikiPathway' (Marvin et al. (2020) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkaa1024>), 'MsigDb' (Arthur et al. (2015) <doi:10.1016/j.cels.2015.12.004>), 'Reactome' (David et al. (2011) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkq1018>), 'MeSH' (Ish et al. (2014) <doi:10.4103/0019-5413.139827>), 'DisGeNET' (Janet et al. (2017) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkw943>), 'Disease Ontology' (Lynn et al. (2011) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkr972>), 'Network of Cancer Genes' (Dimitra et al. (2019) <doi:10.1186/s13059-018-1612-0>) and 'COVID-19' (Maxim et al. (2020) <doi:10.21203/rs.3.rs-28582/v1>). Gene sets are stored in the list object which provides data frame of 'geneset' and 'geneset_name'. The 'geneset' has two columns of term ID and gene ID. The 'geneset_name' has two columns of terms ID and term description.

name: r-geneset
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '0.2.7' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-geneset
  version: "{{ version|replace('-', '_') }}"

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/geneset_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/geneset/geneset_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 868e67ef1bc2344591141bd27a3c41ab580a0e04b5f07064eb15ffe235cd3eba

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-rcurl
    - r-dplyr
    - r-fst
    - r-stringi
    - r-stringr
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-rcurl
    - r-dplyr
    - r-fst
    - r-stringi
    - r-stringr

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('geneset')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('geneset')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://github.com/GangLiLab/geneset
  license: GPL-3.0-only
  summary: Gene sets are fundamental for gene enrichment analysis. The package 'geneset' enables
    querying gene sets from public databases including 'GO' (Gene Ontology Consortium.
    (2004) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkh036>), 'KEGG' (Minoru et al. (2000) <doi:10.1093/nar/28.1.27>),
    'WikiPathway' (Marvin et al. (2020) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkaa1024>), 'MsigDb' (Arthur
    et al. (2015) <doi:10.1016/j.cels.2015.12.004>), 'Reactome' (David et al. (2011)
    <doi:10.1093/nar/gkq1018>), 'MeSH' (Ish et al. (2014) <doi:10.4103/0019-5413.139827>),
    'DisGeNET' (Janet et al. (2017) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkw943>), 'Disease Ontology' (Lynn
    et al. (2011) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkr972>), 'Network of Cancer Genes' (Dimitra et al.
    (2019) <doi:10.1186/s13059-018-1612-0>) and 'COVID-19' (Maxim et al. (2020) <doi:10.21203/rs.3.rs-28582/v1>).
    Gene sets are stored in the list object which provides data frame of 'geneset' and
    'geneset_name'. The 'geneset' has two columns of term ID and gene ID. The 'geneset_name'
    has two columns of terms ID and term description.
  license_family: GPL3
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/GPL-3'

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

(2015) <doi:10.1016/j.cels.2015.12.004>), 'Reactome' (David et al. (2011) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkq1018>), 'MeSH' (Ish et al. (2014) <doi:10.4103/0019-5413.139827>), 'DisGeNET' (Janet et al. (2017) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkw943>), 'Disease Ontology' (Lynn et al. (2011) <doi:10.1093/nar/gkr972>), 'Network of Cancer Genes' (Dimitra et al. (2019) <doi:10.1186/s13059-018-1612-0>) and 'COVID-19' (Maxim et al. (2020) <doi:10.21203/rs.3.rs-28582/v1>). Gene sets are stored in the list object which provides data frame of 'geneset' and 'geneset_name'. The 'geneset' has two columns of term ID and gene ID. The 'geneset_name' has two columns of terms ID and term description.
